concept_ref: §3 row 2 — 'Statement coverage guarantees branch coverage' (corrected by Branch testing)

scenario.title: The Empty Loop
scenario.narrative: A batch processor walks through a list of items. The QA engineer ran one test with a non-empty list, saw every line execute, and signed off.

conditions.label: items is non-empty (loop body executed at least once)

claim: Which is the STRONGEST coverage level the test set below actually satisfies?
hints: A `for` (or `while`) loop has a TRUE edge (enter the body) and a FALSE edge (skip the body / exit immediately). Re-read Law Card 2.
T1 enters the loop body — so the TRUE edge is covered and every line runs. But which edge is NOT covered? What test would exercise it?

Solution — Statement: 100% (every line executed). Branch: 50% (the 'empty list' false-edge of the `for` is never taken). Strongest satisfied level = STATEMENT only. Add T2 with items=[] to reach Branch coverage.

seeded_faults.description: process_all returns the wrong default (e.g. None) when items is empty.

misconceptions.explanation_md: Statement coverage does NOT guarantee Branch coverage. A loop that is entered at least once executes every line, satisfying Statement testing — but the implicit 'skip the loop' edge stays untested. To detect empty-list bugs you must add a test that exercises the FALSE edge of the loop predicate.
